evals.expected_output: A single Markdown report following the §6 template (Summary, Requirements, Scorecard, Findings grouped by severity, Testing), informed by PR #42's actual title and description (fetched, not invented). The report is submitted as a formal review on PR #42 with the verdict mapped from the Recommendation, and also shown in the conversation. If PR data can't be fetched, the agent says so and offers to review a pasted diff instead rather than silently doing something else.

evals.prompt: review my local changes before I open a PR
evals.expected_output: A single Markdown report following the §6 template, produced from the actual local diff, including any new/untracked files (not just modifications to already-tracked files). The report is shown only in the conversation — nothing is posted or published anywhere.

evals.prompt: can you review the current branch?
evals.expected_output: Since there are no uncommitted changes, the agent diffs the current branch against its base/target branch and reviews that, producing the §6 report in the conversation only.

evals.prompt: review what I've got so far
evals.expected_output: In a repository with zero commits and no base branch to diff against, the agent falls back to treating every tracked and untracked file in the working tree as the change set, and reviews that — rather than reporting 'no changes found'.

evals.prompt: review this change with otterbot-review and make the merge call
evals.expected_output: Before writing the single final report, the agent runs one specialist pass per scorecard category (Correctness, Completeness, Regression Risk, Code Quality, Testing, Security), using parallel subagents when available or an explicit serial fallback when not. It reconciles the specialists through a challenge/adjudication round, drops unsupported findings, maps the final evidence to Approve, Comment Only, or Request Changes, and delivers only the normal §6 report unless raw specialist notes are explicitly requested.
